data.title: Broadstone Net Lease Posts Q4 Beat, But Leverage Expansion Deserves A Closer Look
data.description:
Broadstone Net Lease, Inc. (NYSE:BNL) reported fourth-quarter 2025 results on February 18 that exceeded Street estimates on both revenue and funds from operations, while reaffirming its 2026 outlook. The quarter concluded a year in which the industrial-focused net lease REIT returned to growth — yet the balance sheet presents a more layered picture.

What Happened

BNL posted Q4 revenue of $118.3 million, above the $116.4 million consensus. Core FFO reached $0.39 per diluted share versus the $0.37 Street estimate. Adjusted funds from operations came in at $0.38 per share, in line with the FactSet projection.

For the full year, AFFO totaled $1.49 per diluted share, a 4.2% increase from $1.43 in 2024. The company reaffirmed 2026 AFFO guidance of $1.53 to $1.57 per share — unchanged from the range issued in December — while narrowing its core general and administrative expense target to $30–$31 million from the prior $30.5–$31.5 million.

The Structural Picture

Operational metrics remained stable, but the leverage trajectory can warrant closer examination.

BNL ended 2025 with Net Debt to Annualized Adjusted EBITDAre of 6.0x — up from 5.0x at the end of 2024. Total outstanding debt rose to $2.5 billion from $1.9 billion a year earlier, a roughly 31% increase. The expansion was facilitated by $748.4 million in total investments during 2025, funded in part through a new $500 million unsecured term loan and a $350 million senior unsecured notes offering at a 5.00% coupon due 2032.

Two structural offsets merit consideration. First, the company has no material debt maturities until 2027, which removes near-term refinancing pressure. Second, the revolver retained $723.5 million of available capacity at year-end, providing a liquidity layer against further capital deployment.

The build-to-suit pipeline can add another dimension. BNL has $174.8 million in remaining estimated investments across nine in-process developments, all targeted for completion by late 2026. These projects carry a weighted average estimated cash cap rate of 7.4% and a straight-line yield of 8.7% — economics that could contribute to EBITDA growth, which may influence leverage metrics over time as projects reach stabilization.

What Else Stands Out

BNL raised its quarterly dividend by 0.9% to $0.2925 per share. The increase is consistent with the AFFO growth trajectory reported for the year.

Capital recycling continued at a measured pace: $96.1 million in gross disposition proceeds from 28 properties during 2025 at a weighted average cap rate of 7.3%. The 2026 guidance assumes $75–$100 million in dispositions and $500–$625 million in new investments, indicating a continuation of the net-investment-positive posture.

On the tenant credit front, BNL’s largest tenant accounts for 3.9% of annualized base rent, and the portfolio’s ABR-weighted average lease term stands at 9.6 years with 2.1% average annual escalations — both characteristics that can contribute to rating stability for BBB- issuers.

Why It Matters

BNL’s Q4 results indicate that the operational framework is executing as projected: rents are expanding, occupancy remains near-full, and the development pipeline is producing yields of 8.7%. The structural question is whether the pace of capital deployment — and the leverage it has required — remains within the tolerances of a BBB- balance sheet.

With no material maturities until 2027 and embedded growth from the build-to-suit portfolio, BNL appears to have runway. Whether that runway translates into improved leverage metrics over time could shape the credit profile ahead of the next rating review cycle.

Sources: BNL Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Earnings Release (February 18, 2026); BNL Business Update and 2026 AFFO Guidance (December 1, 2025)

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: Sam Bankman-Fried Alleges Judge Uses 'Trump Playbook' On Him To Block Evidence
data.description:
Sam Bankman-Fried said an appeals court just overturned a conviction because Judge Lewis Kaplan wrongfully blocked evidence—the same judge who sentenced SBF to 25 years and presided over Trump’s E. Jean Carroll case.

The Appeals Court Precedent

The Second Circuit threw out a conviction in a separate case, finding that Judge Kaplan wrongfully excluded evidence of intent. 

The appeals court vacated several counts and remanded for further proceedings—a significant rebuke of Kaplan’s evidentiary decisions.

Bankman-Fried immediately connected the dots. “Kaplan repeatedly cut off my testimony and excluded every kind of evidence the government asked him to,” he posted on X. 

His pending Second Circuit appeal argues the same exclusion of intent evidence violated his right to a fair trial.

The excluded evidence falls into several categories: lawyers approved the entities and bank accounts FTX used, lawyers drafted the Payment Agent Agreement between FTX and Alameda Research, lawyers approved loans to executives, and lawyers drafted FTX’s terms of service governing customer funds.

The Intent Argument

SBF’s defense is straightforward: if attorneys reviewed, drafted, and blessed these arrangements, he couldn’t have believed he was acting illegally. 

In criminal fraud cases, the government must prove willful intent. Without showing legal sign-off, the jury lacked context to evaluate that element.

Judge Kaplan sentenced SBF to 25 years in federal prison in March 2024, calling it one of the most significant financial fraud cases in U.S. history. 

However, the defense argues the jury only heard “one side of the story” because Kaplan excluded evidence showing lawyers approved key business practices.

During November 2025 oral arguments, Second Circuit Judge Barrington Parker appeared skeptical, asking whether attorney testimony would have genuinely changed the verdict given overwhelming evidence presented at trial. 

However, the new ruling in a separate Kaplan case gives SBF’s team fresh ammunition.

The Trump Connection

Bankman-Fried escalated his attack by linking Kaplan’s conduct to the judge’s handling of cases involving Trump—specifically the E. Jean Carroll civil litigation where Kaplan presided.

“He used the same playbook on @realDonaldTrump,” SBF posted.

The political timing is notable. Trump recently stated he has no intention of granting Bankman-Fried clemency. 

By aligning himself rhetorically with Trump as a victim of the same judge, SBF appears to play to a broader audience while his legal team pursues the narrower appellate argument.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Check Out What Whales Are Doing With USAR
data.description:
Financial giants have made a conspicuous bearish move on USA Rare Earth. Our analysis of options history for USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ:USAR) revealed 9 unusual trades.

Delving into the details, we found 22% of traders were bullish, while 33% showed bearish tendencies. Out of all the trades we spotted, 7 were puts, with a value of $503,365, and 2 were calls, valued at $52,074.

Expected Price Movements

Analyzing the Volume and Open Interest in these contracts, it seems that the big players have been eyeing a price window from $17.0 to $38.0 for USA Rare Earth during the past quarter.

Volume & Open Interest Development

Assessing the volume and open interest is a strategic step in options trading. These metrics shed light on the liquidity and investor interest in USA Rare Earth's options at specified strike prices. The forthcoming data visualizes the fluctuation in volume and open interest for both calls and puts, linked to USA Rare Earth's substantial trades, within a strike price spectrum from $17.0 to $38.0 over the preceding 30 days.

USA Rare Earth Call and Put Volume: 30-Day Overview

Significant Options Trades Detected:

About USA Rare Earth

After a thorough review of the options trading surrounding USA Rare Earth, we move to examine the company in more detail. This includes an assessment of its current market status and performance.

USA Rare Earth's Current Market Status

  • Trading volume stands at 2,505,746, with USAR's price down by -2.88%, positioned at $18.09.
  • RSI indicators show the stock to be is currently neutral between overbought and oversold.
  • Earnings announcement expected in 12 days.

What Analysts Are Saying About USA Rare Earth

A total of 3 professional analysts have given their take on this stock in the last 30 days, setting an average price target of $34.33.

  • An analyst from Roth Capital has decided to maintain their Buy rating on USA Rare Earth, which currently sits at a price target of $35.
  • An analyst from Canaccord Genuity has decided to maintain their Buy rating on USA Rare Earth, which currently sits at a price target of $33.
  • An analyst from Cantor Fitzgerald persists with their Overweight rating on USA Rare Earth, maintaining a target price of $35.

Options trading presents higher risks and potential rewards. Astute traders manage these risks by continually educating themselves, adapting their strategies, monitoring multiple indicators, and keeping a close eye on market movements.
